summary:
Given an integer n, produce every distinct string of n pairs of parentheses that is well-formed (correctly balanced).

Return all such combinations in a list. The order of the strings in the returned list does not matter.

examples.explanation: With two pairs of parentheses there are exactly two valid arrangements.
All five balanced strings using three pairs of parentheses.

constraints: Every returned string must use exactly n opening and n closing parentheses
Each returned string must be well-formed (balanced)
The list may be returned in any order
